verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I transmembrane protein with documented dual localization to both ER-Golgi and plasma membrane compartments. The lumenal/extracellular domain (Thr91-Glu110) is functionally active, mediating direct interactions with cell-surface TGF-β receptors (ALK5, TβRII), demonstrating that the protein body reaches the outer face of the PM via its own TM-anchored mechanism. While the dominant compartment is ER-Golgi (involved in vesicular trafficking), a documented minority pool localizes stably to the plasma membrane, enabling surface engagement with extracellular ligands and receptors.
